findings.quote: Party A irrevocably undertakes that Party A will take Party B as its exclusive and sole partner to provide the business consultancy and technical services as well as technical consultancy to Party A and its subsidiaries. Party A shall not establish any same or similar cooperative relationship with any third party in respect of such businesses nor shall it make any same or similar arrangement, unless with the prior written consent of Party B.
findings.why: Party A is required to deal exclusively with Party B for the relevant services and cannot engage any third-party substitute.

findings.quote: Party A irrevocably undertakes that, without Party B's consent, Party A shall not conduct any other business or make any commercial arrangement, including without limitation being engaged in or otherwise participating in any commercial activities and businesses independently or together with any other person or entity, nor shall it carry out any activities that may be competitive with or cause adverse effect to Party B's business.
findings.why: Bars Party A from conducting competing or otherwise unapproved commercial activities without Party B's consent.

findings.quote: if Party A or its subsidiaries fail to pay the Service Fee in full on schedule according to provisions of Article 4.1 and Article 4.2, then Party A or its subsidiaries shall, in addition to the continuance of the payment of Service Fee in full, it shall pay Party B the liquidated damages at a daily interest rate of 0.03% in respect of the outstanding Service Fee.
findings.why: Pre-agreed daily-rate formula payable on late payment, independent of actual proven loss.

findings.quote: Party B shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in advance without the prior written consent from Party A, bysending a written notice to Party A but Party A may not terminate or rescind this Agreement;
findings.why: Party B may terminate at will on notice without cause; Party A has no reciprocal termination right.

findings.quote: The Parties agree that any and all intellectual property researched and developed, created and invented by the Parties (including their employees) in the course of performance of this Agreement shall be owned by Party B. For the purpose of this Article 12.3, "Intellectual Property" means the patent, patent application right, trademark, service mark, logo, image, trade name, internet domain name, design right, copyright (including copyright of computer software) and moral rights, database right, right of semiconductor design drawing, utility model, proprietary technology and other intellectual property that are registered and unregistered including those that have applied for registration, as well as all other rights or protection methods with same or similar effect on a global scope.
findings.why: All IP created by either party (including Party A's employees) is assigned entirely to Party B.

findings.quote: The rights and obligations of each Party under this Agreement shall not be transferred, except for the transfer by Party B to its affiliates.
findings.why: Assignment is restricted; only Party B may transfer its rights (to affiliates), Party A cannot assign at all.

findings.quote: Each Party shall be responsible for and hold the other Party harmless from any loss, damages and claim arising out of violation of anyrepresentations and warranties hereunder.
findings.why: Mutual indemnity for warranty breach with no cap stated, exposing each party to unlimited liability for such breaches.

findings.quote: Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions, neither Party shall be responsible to the other Party in respect of any indirect loss or damage caused hereunder.
findings.why: Excludes indirect damages from liability — identifies the boundary of what is uncapped (direct losses remain uncapped since no cap clause exists).

findings.quote: Party A irrevocably undertakes that it will make best efforts to assist and endeavor to achieve the exclusive operation of thecooperative business to the extent permitted by laws.
findings.why: Reinforces exclusive cooperation obligation by requiring Party A to actively work toward exclusive operation.
